title: Introduction to Image Captioning
concept: Image captioning bridges computer vision and natural language processing to generate descriptive text sentences for input images.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is horizontal reading order bad for multi-column documents?

options.text: It reads across columns, blending unrelated sentences together.
It disables the GPU graphics card.
It converts text strings back to binary values.

exercises.explain: Correct! Simple horizontal line scanning reads across column boundaries, scrambled reading blocks. Layout analysis is needed first.

cards.title: Describing the world in words
cards.body: If you show a computer a photo of a dog catching a frisbee, a classifier might output: `[dog, frisbee, grass]`. But a list of tags doesn't capture the scene's action. To translate the visual world into human experiences, the AI must describe the relationships: 'A brown dog jumps to catch a yellow frisbee in the park.' This is image captioning.

cards.title: What is Image Captioning?
cards.body: Image Captioning is the task of automatically generating a coherent natural language description (a caption) for an input image. It bridges Computer Vision (to extract visual patterns) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) (to assemble words grammatically).

cards.title: A translator for art
cards.body: Imagine a gallery visitor who cannot see. An art translator looks at the painting, identifies the subjects and their actions, and speaks a structured, descriptive sentence in English. The translator doesn't just list items; they explain the story happening on the canvas.

cards.title: Accessibility alt-text
cards.body: Social media platforms use image captioning to automatically generate 'alt-text' for photos uploaded by users. Screen readers read these descriptions aloud, allowing visually impaired users to understand what their friends are posting.

cards.title: Not just multi-label classification
cards.body: A common myth is that captioning is just labeling objects. In reality, a caption must model verbs, prepositions, and syntax. Listing 'man, horse, lasso' is not the same as explaining 'A cowboy is chasing a wild horse with a lasso.'

cards.title: Tags vs. Captions
cards.body: Comparing classification tags to image captioning:

left.text:
Outputs tags: [cat, laptop, desk]
No relational structure
Missing action/context

right.text:
Outputs sentence: 'A cat is sleeping on a laptop keyboard.'
Explains relationships
Grammatically complete

exercises.prompt: What two domains of artificial intelligence does image captioning bridge?

options.text: Computer Vision and Natural Language Processing

exercises.explain: Image captioning requires computer vision to understand pixels and NLP to generate grammatical sentences.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Image captioning is simply multi-label classification, as it only lists the objects present in an image.
exercises.explain: False. Captioning must generate structured, grammatical sentences that describe actions and relationships, not just flat lists of tags.

exercises.sentence: Image captioning maps visual pixels directly into natural ___ descriptions.
exercises.explain: The goal of captioning is to translate visual scenes into human language (sentences).

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs represents an image captioning model's prediction?

options.text: Sentence: 'A little boy is playing in a sandbox with a shovel.'
Bounding Boxes: [boy at x=20, shovel at x=40]

exercises.explain: A complete grammatical sentence describing the scene is the output of a captioning model.

exercises.prompt: You are building an accessibility feature for a photo-sharing app. It must help visually impaired users understand the content and action of photos. Which technology should you deploy?

options.text: An image captioning model, to generate detailed, grammatical descriptions of what is happening in the photos.
A face recognition model, to tell the user the name of the camera model.
A classical edge detector, to outline the shapes on the screen.
A style transfer model, to make the photos look like oil paintings.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Image captioning translates visual content and actions into structured sentences, which screen readers can read aloud to describe the scene. Outstanding! Next: the classical CNN-RNN architecture.
